description: Remote Attach Session Sharing - Multi-client collaboration with owner/observer roles, reconnect tokens, and unified TUI architecture

userStories.title: Worker lock file module
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a lock file module that handles Worker discovery, creation, validation, and cleanup so that TUI and Worker processes can coordinate.
acceptanceCriteria: Create lock file module at packages/opencode/src/server/lockfile.ts
Path convention: $XDG_DATA_HOME/opencode/<dir-hash>/worker.lock where dir-hash encodes absolute path (/ replaced with _), truncated + SHA-256 suffix if over 255 bytes
Lock file format: JSON with pid, port, token, createdAt fields — validated with Zod schema
Lock file permissions set to 0600 (owner-only read/write)
Atomic creation using O_EXCL or equivalent to prevent TOCTOU races
Read function validates JSON structure and returns parsed lock data
Stale lock detection: kill -0 PID check, clean up if process not alive
Delete function for cleanup on Worker exit
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Pure utility module, no server/Worker changes yet. See PRD Technical Considerations → Lock File 规格 for full spec. Use Bun.file() for reads, node:fs for O_EXCL atomic write.

userStories.title: Worker detached process launcher
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Worker to run as a detached background process so that TUI can exit without killing the server.
acceptanceCriteria: Worker starts as detached process via Bun.spawn() with detached: true and stdio: ignore, then calls process.unref() to ensure parent TUI process can exit cleanly
Worker accepts --mode=auto|serve startup parameter (auto for TUI-launched, serve for opencode serve)
Worker binds HTTP server to localhost:random-port, then atomically creates lock file with pid + port + token + createdAt
If lock file atomic creation fails (another Worker won the race), Worker closes its HTTP server and exits
TUI startup checks for existing lock file: if valid Worker running, connect to it; if stale, clean up and start new Worker
TUI waits for lock file to appear after spawning Worker, reads port and token from it
Worker registers SIGTERM/SIGINT handlers that clean up lock file before exit
opencode stop command reads lock file PID and sends SIGTERM
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Modifies worker.ts to become a detached process model. TUI thread.ts connects via HTTP using lock file port. Does NOT yet remove RPC paths — that's the next story.

userStories.title: Remove in-process RPC and unify to HTTP/SSE
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to remove the dual code paths (RPC + HTTP) so all TUI communication goes through HTTP/SSE uniformly.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove createWorkerFetch() and createEventSource() in-process RPC code paths from thread.ts
Local TUI connects to Worker HTTP server via standard HTTP/SSE (same as remote attach)
sdk.tsx simplified to use only SSE path (remove custom event source branch)
Remove worker.ts redundant SSE reconnect loop (fixed 250ms retry) — SDK-side exponential backoff handles reconnection
Add .catch() protection around sdk.tsx's sdk.event.subscribe() call to prevent loop termination on subscribe errors
Preserve existing server.instance.disposed → bootstrap() trigger chain during RPC removal refactoring
Local TUI as first client connects via HTTP/SSE and becomes first attach client
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the core architecture unification. After this, all TUI (local and remote) use the same HTTP/SSE path. sdk.tsx abstraction layer means TUI components should not need changes. Must preserve existing server.instance.disposed → bootstrap() chain in sync.tsx.

userStories.title: Worker lifecycle modes and idle watchdog
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Worker lifecycle rules so auto-launched Workers clean up when idle and serve-mode Workers persist.
acceptanceCriteria: --mode=auto: Worker auto-exits when all clients disconnect and grace period (60s) expires with no reconnects
--mode=auto: Idle watchdog — if no active SSE connections AND no active sessions (busy/retry) for 5 minutes, Worker self-exits and cleans lock file
--mode=serve (opencode serve): Worker never auto-exits regardless of client connections; no idle watchdog
opencode serve runs Worker as foreground process (not detached), with its own SIGINT/SIGTERM handlers, writes lock file, listens on port
opencode serve supports --port parameter to specify a fixed port (e.g., opencode serve --port 8080); default is random port
Mutual exclusion: if lock file exists with live process, opencode serve exits with error; TUI connects to existing Worker
Worker accepts --hostname parameter to bind to specified address (default localhost); opencode serve supports --hostname 0.0.0.0 for network exposure
Define shared TIMEOUT constant (60_000ms) for reuse by later ownership stories
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-002 (detached process model). Grace period timeout constant should be defined as a shared constant (TIMEOUT = 60_000) for reuse by later ownership stories. opencode serve is a foreground process (not detached) — distinct from TUI-launched detached Workers.

userStories.title: Auth token middleware for network exposure
userStories.description: As a server admin, when I expose the server on the network via --hostname 0.0.0.0, I need auth middleware to prevent unauthorized access.
acceptanceCriteria: When --hostname is not localhost/127.0.0.1/::1, server auto-generates a random UUID auth token and prints it to terminal
User can specify custom token via --auth-token <token> parameter
Auth token written to lock file (token field); when --hostname is localhost/127.0.0.1/::1, token field is null
Auth check implemented as Hono middleware, runs before any other route checks
Client sends token via Authorization: Bearer <token> header
Server validates token on all requests (SSE + REST); mismatch returns HTTP 401 Unauthorized
When --hostname 0.0.0.0, ALL connections (including local 127.0.0.1) require auth token — auth based on server bind hostname not client IP
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Focus on server-side middleware only. CLI parameter --auth-token for 'opencode attach' is handled in US-006.

userStories.title: Local TUI auto-auth and attach --auth-token
userStories.description: As a user, local TUI should auto-authenticate via lock file token, and remote attach should support --auth-token parameter.
acceptanceCriteria: Local TUI reads token from lock file automatically and passes it as Authorization: Bearer header (transparent to user)
opencode attach <url> supports --auth-token <token> CLI parameter for remote connections
SDK client wrapper (v2/client.ts) supports optional authToken parameter passed through to Authorization header
No modifications to auto-generated code (gen/ directory)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-005 (auth middleware). Modifies thread.ts for local TUI and SDK wrapper for auth header injection.

userStories.title: update_seq DB migration
userStories.description: As a developer, I need update_seq columns on mutable entities so clients can detect stale data.
acceptanceCriteria: Session table gets new update_seq integer column via DB migration (default value 0)
Message table gets new update_seq integer column via DB migration (default value 0)
Part table gets new update_seq integer column via DB migration (default value 0)
Migration uses IF NOT EXISTS pattern
Export updated table definitions from src/storage/schema.ts if needed
Generate Drizzle migration with: cd packages/opencode && bun drizzle-kit generate --name add-update-seq
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Session table has existing 'version' column storing software version string like '0.0.3' — cannot be reused. time_updated is millisecond timestamp which isn't monotonically incrementing for same-ms writes. This is schema-only — incrementing logic comes in next story.

userStories.title: update_seq increment and API/SSE integration
userStories.description: As a developer, I need update_seq to increment on every write and be included in API responses and SSE events.
acceptanceCriteria: Each entity independently maintains its own update_seq (per-entity counter, not global)
Every write to session, message, and part tables increments update_seq
All REST API responses include entity's update_seq field
All SSE broadcast events (message.updated, session.status, etc.) include entity's update_seq field
After server restart, persisted entity update_seq recovered from DB (SELECT MAX(update_seq) FROM <table> WHERE id = ?)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-007 (migration). Non-persisted state (client list, ownership) will use server in-memory per-entity incrementing counters — added in later stories. This story touches every write path for session/message/part + all API responses + all SSE events — if too large for one iteration, consider splitting into 'session update_seq' and 'message/part update_seq'.

userStories.title: Client tracking and server.connected event extension
userStories.description: As a server, I need to track connected clients with unique IDs so that ownership and reconnection can work.
acceptanceCriteria: SSE /event connection assigns unique clientID (UUID) to each client
Server maintains in-memory ConnectedClients Map: clientID → { directory, connectedAt, reconnectToken, role, type, remoteIP }
Client type detected from X-OpenCode-Client-Type header (tui or cli); remote IP extracted from request
server.connected inline event properties extended with: clientID, reconnectToken, role ('owner' or 'observer'), ownerClientID, timeout (unified TIMEOUT constant in ms)
First client to connect automatically becomes instance owner (ownerClientID set in memory)
New broadcast events registered via BusEvent.define: client.connected, client.disconnected
Non-persisted state (client list entries) maintains in-memory per-entity update_seq counters for SSE event versioning
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Core client infrastructure. server.connected stays as connection-level inline event (not broadcast). client.connected/disconnected are broadcast-level events. Reconnect token and grace period logic is in next story. Non-persisted state update_seq counters are simple in-memory integers, incremented on each change.

userStories.title: Reconnect token and grace period
userStories.description: As a server, I need reconnect tokens so that disconnected clients can recover their identity within a grace period.
acceptanceCriteria: Server generates reconnectToken (UUID) for each client, returned in server.connected event
Client reconnection via X-OpenCode-Reconnect-Token header: server validates token, reuses old clientID, issues new reconnectToken
Reconnect token is one-time use: after successful reconnect, old token invalidated, new token issued
Client disconnect enters grace period (TIMEOUT constant, default 60s); reconnect within grace period restores identity
Grace period timeout with no reconnect: clean up client record, broadcast client.disconnected event
During grace period after owner disconnect, new clients join as observer (owner may reconnect)
When all clients disconnect AND grace period expires, ownerClientID resets to null; next client becomes owner
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-009 (client tracking). This adds reconnect token validation and grace period timer to the client tracking infrastructure. Note: TIMEOUT constant (60s) is shared across grace period, activity timeout, idle timeout, and takeover cooldown. Do not exceed 60s — grace period also determines takeover condition C wait time; too long means observers can't operate the instance after owner disconnects.

userStories.title: GET /clients API endpoint
userStories.description: As a client, I need an API to query the list of connected clients and their roles.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /clients API returns: ownerClientID, takeoverAvailable (hardcode false for now), clients array
Each client entry: clientID, role, type, remoteIP, connectedAt, duration (server-computed)
Response validated with Zod schema
Only returns clients for the same directory as the requesting client
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-009 (client tracking). takeoverAvailable will be computed properly when takeover endpoint is implemented.

userStories.title: SDK clientID header injection and ready gate
userStories.description: As an SDK client, I need to receive my clientID from server.connected and inject it into all subsequent requests.
acceptanceCriteria: sdk.tsx intercepts server.connected event in SSE stream, extracts clientID, reconnectToken, role, ownerClientID into shared state
SDK wrapper (v2/client.ts) extended to support dynamic header values (function-form headers) for X-OpenCode-Client-ID and X-OpenCode-Reconnect-Token
No modifications to auto-generated code (gen/ directory)
All subsequent requests include X-OpenCode-Client-ID header

SSE connections (first + reconnect) include X-OpenCode-Client-ID (if available), X-OpenCode-Reconnect-Token, and X-OpenCode-Client-Type headers — X-OpenCode-Client-ID on SSE is used for server restart owner recovery (US-026)
Ready gate: write operations (non-GET) wait for clientID to be available before sending; GET requests not gated
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-009 (server-side client tracking). config.headers in v2/client.ts is currently static — needs extension to support dynamic/function-form values.

userStories.title: Server-side clientID validation middleware
userStories.description: As a server, I need to validate that incoming requests carry a valid clientID from a connected client.
acceptanceCriteria: Write requests without X-OpenCode-Client-ID return HTTP 400 with 'upgrade SDK' hint
GET requests don't require clientID (allows debugging and monitoring tools)
Server validates clientID exists in ConnectedClients Map; invalid clientID returns HTTP 403 (distinct from auth 401)
Validation implemented as Hono middleware, runs after auth check but before route handlers
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-009 (ConnectedClients Map). This middleware enforces that write operations come from known clients.

userStories.title: Instance-level owner/observer role model
userStories.description: As a system, I need a per-instance owner model where exactly one client has write access and others are read-only observers.
acceptanceCriteria: Instance gets in-memory ownerClientID field (nullable), tracking which client has write permission
First client to connect becomes owner (coordinated with US-009)
Owner has write permission to all sessions in the instance including child sessions (ultrawork loop, subagent task)
Child session prompts (server-initiated) bypass ownership checks
Ownership transfers only via takeover or all-clients-disconnect + grace-period-timeout
New broadcast event: instance.owner.changed (via BusEvent.define), includes ownerClientID and in-memory update_seq counter
All clients receive owner change notifications via SSE
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-009/010 (client tracking + reconnect). This establishes the ownership model without the enforcement (US-015) or takeover (US-017/018). Ownership state uses in-memory update_seq counter (non-persisted) for SSE event versioning consistency.

userStories.title: Observer write operation interception (assertCanWrite)
userStories.description: As an observer, when I try to send a message, the system should return a clear 423 Locked error instead of an ambiguous error.
acceptanceCriteria: assertNotBusy enhanced to assertCanWrite(clientID): first checks instance ownership (non-owner → HTTP 423 Locked), then existing busy/retry logic unchanged
423 response includes ownerClientID so client knows who is operating
Observer requests return 423 immediately — do NOT enter callback queue
Child session internal prompts (server-initiated) bypass assertCanWrite entirely
Permission reply and question reply endpoints also check owner — non-owner gets 423
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-014. Modifies prompt.ts assertNotBusy → assertCanWrite. Server-side enforcement — TUI-side UI changes come later.

userStories.title: Owner activity heartbeat endpoint
userStories.description: As a server, I need to know if the owner is actively using the instance so that takeover conditions can be evaluated.
acceptanceCriteria: New POST /instance/activity endpoint accepting { active: boolean } body
Only owner can call this — non-owner returns HTTP 403
Server records lastReport timestamp (any activity call) and lastActiveReport timestamp (last active: true call) in memory
TUI owner sends activity heartbeat every 10s with active field reflecting recent user interaction (input, keypress, focus)
OwnerState interface: { clientID, lastReport, lastActiveReport, takeoverAvailable }
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-014. Prerequisite for takeover conditions. Uses separate POST endpoint (not SSE) because SSE is server→client only.

userStories.title: Observer takeover endpoint core logic
userStories.description: As an observer, when the owner is inactive or disconnected, I can take over instance ownership.
acceptanceCriteria: New POST /instance/takeover endpoint accepting { force?: boolean }

Takeover available when ANY condition met: (A) owner activity report timeout (lastReport > TIMEOUT), (B) all sessions idle AND owner lastActiveReport > TIMEOUT, (C) owner disconnected and grace period expired
Conditions not met → HTTP 409 { reason: 'owner_active', ownerClientID }
Active sessions (busy/retry) without force → HTTP 409 { reason: 'active_sessions', ownerClientID, sessions: [...] }
Takeover success with active sessions + force: abort all active session prompts, mark assistant messages incomplete
Takeover success: set ownerClientID to requester, publish instance.owner.changed event
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-016 (activity heartbeat). retry status treated same as busy for all takeover logic. See PRD pseudocode for handleTakeover flow. Full /instance/takeover API schema has 4 different 409 response variants: { reason: 'cooldown', retryAfter } (US-018), { reason: 'owner_active', ownerClientID }, { reason: 'active_sessions', ownerClientID, sessions }, { reason: 'owner_changed', ownerClientID } (US-018). Success 200: { ownerClientID }.

userStories.title: Takeover cooldown, race handling, and availability event
userStories.description: As a system, I need takeover cooldown to prevent ownership ping-pong, race handling for concurrent takeover, and availability broadcasting.
acceptanceCriteria: Cooldown period after takeover (TIMEOUT constant) — no takeover allowed during cooldown → 409 { reason: 'cooldown', retryAfter }
Concurrent takeover race: first succeeds, second gets 409 { reason: 'owner_changed', ownerClientID }
Owner can takeover self (for reconnect token expiry recovery), also subject to cooldown
Server publishes takeover.available broadcast event (via BusEvent.define) when availability changes (false→true or true→false)

Server schedules a timer after each owner activity report; if no new report arrives within TIMEOUT, re-evaluate takeover conditions and broadcast takeover.available if state changed. Also re-evaluate on discrete events (client disconnect, activity report, session status change)
GET /clients includes takeoverAvailable field computed server-side (update the hardcoded false from US-011)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-017 (takeover core). Adds cooldown, race protection, self-takeover, and broadcast event on top of core takeover logic. Conditions A and B are time-based (Date.now() - lastReport > TIMEOUT), so server needs a timer mechanism to detect when these conditions become true due to time passing.

userStories.title: SSE reconnect with identity recovery and bootstrap refresh
userStories.description: As an attach client, when SSE disconnects I need automatic reconnection with identity recovery and full state refresh.
acceptanceCriteria: SSE reconnection uses SDK exponential backoff (3s → 6s → 12s → ... → 30s max), sseMaxRetryAttempts stays undefined (infinite)
Reconnect sends X-OpenCode-Reconnect-Token header automatically
Valid token: server reuses old clientID, owner identity preserved, new token issued
Expired token (grace period passed): server assigns new clientID, client joins as observer
Client resets store on reconnect (clear all cached data and update_seq values)
Reconnect triggers bootstrap() full refresh to compensate for missed events
Bootstrap and SSE events use unified update_seq version check: only apply when incoming.update_seq > stored.update_seq
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-007/008 (update_seq), US-010 (reconnect token), US-012 (SDK headers). sync.tsx needs to listen for server.connected event and trigger bootstrap().

userStories.title: Reconnecting overlay and disconnection UI
userStories.description: As a TUI user, when my connection drops I need clear visual feedback and interaction freeze until reconnected.
acceptanceCriteria: SSE disconnect immediately shows full-screen semi-transparent overlay with 'Reconnecting...' text and countdown timer (timer reflects SDK's current exponential backoff interval)
Overlay freezes all user interaction (prompt input, keyboard shortcuts) to prevent lost operations
Reconnect success automatically dismisses overlay and restores interaction
On reconnect, client resets store (clears all cached data and update_seq values) before triggering bootstrap() — coordinates with US-019 store reset logic
After reconnect, bootstrap() runs and restores full state including: sessions (with child sessions), messages, parts, permissions, questions, todos, diffs, LSP, MCP, formatter, sandbox, vcs status
Client list fetched via GET /clients in non-blocking phase (role info already available from server.connected event)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Depends on US-019 (reconnect mechanism). sync.tsx currently does NOT listen for server.connected event. The complete bootstrap data list must match all data types managed by sync.tsx.

userStories.title: TUI observer mode UI and role display
userStories.description: As an observer, I need clear visual indicators showing my role and disabled write controls.
acceptanceCriteria: TUI sidebar shows current role: 'Owner' or 'Observer'
TUI sidebar shows connected client list with clientID abbreviation and role
Observer mode: prompt input box becomes disabled, shows 'Instance in use by another client' message
Owner/Observer status change has visual transition (e.g., color flash)
Observer sees permission/question dialogs but without interactive shortcuts (view only, with 'View Only' label)
Verify permission.asked, permission.replied, question.asked, and question.replied events are received by observer clients (existing broadcast behavior)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Depends on US-014 (owner model), US-015 (write interception). Modifies TUI sidebar, prompt components, and permission/question dialogs.

userStories.title: TUI takeover button for observers
userStories.description: As an observer, when takeover conditions are met, I can take over ownership from the TUI.
acceptanceCriteria: Observer input area shows 'Takeover' button when takeoverAvailable is true (from takeover.available event or GET /clients)
Takeover button hidden/disabled when conditions not met
Clicking Takeover with active sessions shows confirmation dialog with force warning
Clicking Takeover without active sessions executes immediately (no force needed)
Successful takeover transitions UI to owner mode (prompt enabled, role updated)
Failed takeover shows error message with reason
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Depends on US-017/018 (takeover endpoint), US-021 (observer UI). Listen for takeover.available event to toggle button visibility.

userStories.title: Commands panel Connected Clients detail
userStories.description: As a user, I can view detailed information about all connected clients through the Commands panel.
acceptanceCriteria: Commands panel (Ctrl+P) has new 'Show Connected Clients' command
Command opens dialog showing each client: clientID, role (owner/observer), type (TUI/CLI), remote IP, connected time, duration
Client list updates in real-time (refreshes on client.connected/client.disconnected events)
Current client highlighted with 'You' label
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Depends on US-011 (GET /clients API). Uses client.connected/disconnected events for real-time updates.

userStories.title: Typing status broadcast
userStories.description: As an observer, I want to see when the owner is typing so I know new messages are coming.
acceptanceCriteria: New POST /session/:sessionID/typing endpoint; server returns 200 ok or 403 (non-owner); does not validate sessionID existence
Server publishes session.typing broadcast event (via BusEvent.define) with sessionID and clientID
TUI owner sends typing with 300ms throttle during prompt input, only in existing sessions, only when other clients connected
Client count tracked locally via client.connected/client.disconnected events, initialized from GET /clients
Observer shows 'Owner is typing...' in message area only when viewing the matching session
Typing indicator auto-hides after 2s with no new typing event
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Known limitation: typing not visible for first message in new session (session doesn't exist yet on server).

userStories.title: CLI run --attach owner/observer adaptation
userStories.description: As a CLI user using opencode run --attach, if I'm not the owner I should get a clear error and exit.
acceptanceCriteria: CLI opencode run --attach connects to remote server, performs agent validation (existing logic)
After agent validation, checks role from server.connected event
If role is observer: outputs clear error message including ownerClientID, exits with non-zero code
If role is owner: proceeds with normal prompt execution
During execution, if ownership is taken over (instance.owner.changed event), current prompt is aborted, CLI exits with non-zero code
No --wait blocking functionality (direct exit for observers)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-014 (owner model), US-015 (write interception). Modifies run.ts attach mode.

userStories.title: Server restart owner recovery
userStories.description: As a server, after restart I need to recover the previous owner identity so the original owner can seamlessly reconnect.
acceptanceCriteria: ownerClientID persisted to SQLite (or local file) on every owner change
After server restart, read persisted ownerClientID; enter grace period
During grace period, client connecting with X-OpenCode-Client-ID matching persisted ownerClientID automatically becomes owner (even without valid reconnect token)
Grace period timeout with no matching client: clear persisted record, next connecting client becomes owner
Non-matching clients during grace period join as observer
Security note: this relies on trusted clients (auth token is the trust boundary, not clientID — documented in Non-Goals)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-009/010 and US-014. This is the server restart recovery mechanism. Memory-state counters (update_seq for non-persisted state) restart from 0 — paired with client-side store reset on reconnect.
